message:
results: phase 3 task 5 — compaction behavior verification

Verified that compact and session-log strategies handle compaction
differently at low threshold scales. compact uses standard "checkpoint"
(layer 3) while session-log uses "session_log_checkpoint". Both survive
aggressive compaction and complete the task.

Includes runs at both 0.05 and 0.01 threshold scales.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,51 @@
+Phase 3, Task 5: Compaction Behavior Verification
+==================================================
+
+Model: gpt-4.1-mini
+Task: Read main.py, add is_prime/prime_factors/collatz_steps, write tests, run tests.
+
+Objective: Verify that compact and session-log strategies handle compaction
+differently -- specifically that session-log uses session_log_checkpoint
+(layer 3) instead of the standard checkpoint.
+
+Finding: --threshold-scale 0.1 was insufficient to trigger compaction on this
+short task (25k total tokens across 6 turns, max single-call context ~6-8k out
+of 128k window = ~5% pressure, threshold at 6%). Scale 0.05 triggered only
+Layer 1. Scale 0.01 triggered all four layers and confirmed the key difference.
+
+Results at --threshold-scale 0.05 (gentle compaction)
+-----------------------------------------------------
+  compact:     4 events, layers=[observation_mask x4], completed=true
+  session-log: 3 events, layers=[observation_mask x3], completed=true,
+               fork_summary_calls=6
+
+  At 0.05 scale, only Layer 1 fires. L1 masking reduces context below the
+  L2/L3/L4 thresholds. Both strategies complete the task normally.
+
+Results at --threshold-scale 0.01 (aggressive compaction)
+---------------------------------------------------------
+  compact:     64 events, layers=[observation_mask, thinking_clear, checkpoint,
+               summarize] x16, completed=true
+  session-log: 24 events, layers=[observation_mask, thinking_clear,
+               session_log_checkpoint, summarize] x6, completed=true,
+               fork_summary_calls=6
+
+  At 0.01 scale, all four layers cascade on every turn. This confirms:
+  - compact uses "checkpoint" (deterministic layer 3)
+  - session-log uses "session_log_checkpoint" (session-log-based layer 3)
+  - Both strategies survive aggressive compaction and complete the task
+
+Pass/Fail
+---------
+  [PASS] Both strategies trigger compaction
+  [PASS] compact uses standard layers: observation_mask, thinking_clear,
+         checkpoint, summarize
+  [PASS] session-log uses session_log_checkpoint instead of checkpoint
+  [PASS] Both strategies complete the task after compaction
+  [PASS] session-log fork_summary_calls > 0
+
+Note: --threshold-scale 0.01 is too aggressive for production use (causes
+16 API calls instead of 6 for compact). The 0.05 results show realistic
+compaction behavior where only the lightest layer fires. Future evaluation
+phases should use longer tasks or larger models to naturally trigger higher
+compaction layers.
